title: Area War Card Game Area and Perimeter Practice Game Area of Rectangles
description: Teaching the concept of area and perimeter can sometimes be a challenge. Increase comprehension by making it a game! Who doesn’t love a good game of War? With this area and perimeter war card game , your kids can have all the fun of the classic card game and at the same time reinforce those important concepts! HOW TO PLAY Area War is played just like the traditional card game. The catch is, each time the two players turn over a card they must calculate the area of the rectangle to see who wins the match ! The player with the higher area wins! VARIATION: This game can also be played as PERIMETER WAR! All the same rules apply, just solve for perimeter instead of area! WITH THIS GAME YOU WILL RECEIVE 1 COMPLETE DECK (52) Area War Cards 1 War Rules Instruction Sheet 1 Student Scratch Paper Sheet I hope this year is the most SUPER year yet for you and your students! _____________________________________________________________________________________________ Let’s Stay Connected Click HERE to receive fun FREE classroom resources as well as classroom tips and fun teacher hacks from Tied 2 Teaching! MORE THAN 20 reading passages on voting, presidential eligibility, the history of voting political parties, the office of the president, Joe Biden and Donald Trump, free and fair elections, presidential pets and more , explain hard concepts and help bring understanding about the election process and the 2024 candidates. title: Who Was Aviator Amelia Earhart Biography Lesson Pack Women’s History Unit
description: Have your students ever asked, Who was Amelia Earhart? With our Amelia Earhart Mini Biography Unit, you can guide them on an exciting journey through the life of one of the world’s most famous aviators. This fun mini unit gives you all the tools you need to help your students explore and understand Amelia’s remarkable achievements What You’ll Love To start, you’ll find short, engaging reading passages that capture key moments from Amelia’s life. These passages make it easy for elementary students to grasp important facts while keeping them interested. After reading, they can test their knowledge with a short assessment. Plus, interactive notebook foldables add a hands-on element that keeps learning dynamic and fun. In addition to reading, this unit provides several writing activities that encourage students to reflect on Amelia’s incredible perseverance, bravery, and goal-setting. These activities challenge students to think about how they can apply these qualities in their own lives. For example, in the Vanished Into Thin Air activity, students explore her mysterious disappearance, while I Can Do It Too helps them connect with Amelia’s determination. To enhance your classroom’s visual appeal, the unit includes posters and a timeline, which allow students to track Amelia’s milestones. These make great bulletin board displays and give students a clear sense of her accomplishments over time. The doodle poster adds a creative touch, giving students the chance to combine art and history in a fun way. As students progress, they’ll also engage in thoughtful activities like the Amelia and Me Compare and Contrast, which helps them compare their own characteristics to Amelia’s. In addition, the included Amelia Earhart Quote Poster & Activity gets students thinking about the deeper meaning behind one of her famous quotes. To make lessons even more engaging, you’ll also have access to video links and other resources. These tools bring Amelia’s story to life and give students a richer understanding of her legacy. I hope you’ll find that this Amelia Earhart Mini Biography Unit provides everything you need to make learning about this aviation pioneer engaging, informative, and fun. I know your students will love diving into her story! title: Red Ribbon Week 2024 Activities Just Say No to Drug Awareness Pennants Crafts
description: Looking for a creative and meaningful Red Ribbon Week Say No to Drugs activity? These pennant banners are the perfect way to engage your students while reinforcing the importance of staying drug-free! They are ideal for Red Ribbon Week 2024 or anytime throughout the year,! This Say No to Drugs activity combines drug awareness with literacy instruction. It will provide a well-rounded educational experience for your students. Each pennant is designed to help students explore the significance of making a pledge to remain drug-free. By completing the activity, students actively engage with the concept of drug awareness. They also practice key literacy skills such as writing, self-expression, and critical thinking. Whether you use these pennants during Red Ribbon Week or integrate them into your year-round health curriculum, they are a fun, interactive way to reinforce the importance of a healthy, drug-free lifestyle. One of the greatest features of these pennants is their versatility. They make for an effective and fun classroom activity. They also serve as vibrant decorations for bulletin boards and hallway displays. Imagine walking down the hall and seeing a beautiful display of student-created pennants! They allow each student to share their own unique commitment to staying drug-free. It’s a powerful message that resonates throughout the school, building a sense of community and shared responsibility. This activity offers an ideal blend of creativity and education. While students enjoy designing their pennants, they are also internalizing an important life lesson. Encouraging them to think critically about why they choose to remain drug-free fosters thoughtful discussions and deepens their understanding of the importance of this commitment. Note: The term “Red Ribbon Week” is used with express written permission from the rights holder, ensuring this activity aligns with trademark/copyright considerations. Empower your students to take the pledge to stay drug-free this year with this engaging, educational Red Ribbon Week Say No to Drugs Activity! title: Back to School Goal Setting Activities Students Worksheets First Day of School
description: Help your students keep a growth mindset and set meaningful goals this back to school as you launch into the new school year. It is my gift to you! ⭐️⭐️ NEW YEAR 2025 ONE WORD RESOLUTIONS (UPDATED EVERY YEAR!) Just one word New Year resolutions are the perfect way to help students set meaningful resolutions and goals for the new year. Are you looking for the perfect way to help your students set New Years 2025 resolutions, a fun New Years craft or New Years Bulletin Board, or simply an engaging after winter break activity? title: Valentines Day Party and February Reward Coupons Notes from the Teacher
description: Valentine’s Day incentive reward coupons are awesome to use for incentives, rewards and motivation in your classroom. Each coupon has a fun Valentines theme incentive that students WANT to work for! Kids love getting rewards and recognition and really work hard to earn these incentives. Everyone knows the most motivational rewards are ones that actually mean something to the student! These coupons are kid tested! You can either surprise your student with a coupon of your choice. Or, for further motivation, let them choose their own!!! THESE ALSO MAKE GREAT STUDENT GIFTS! SIMPLY PUT THEM TOGETHER IN A COUPON BOOK GIFT FOR YOUR KIDS AND THEY WILL GO WILD! Each of the 40 coupons comes in full color and black and white for printing convenience. This pack contains 40 different emoji reward coupons: • Homework? Not Today! – one free homework assignment • Sleep In – I get to Skip Morning Work • Puzzler – I get to work on a puzzle of my choice • Running Late – I can turn in an assignment 1 day late • Computer Time – I can plat an educational game for 15 minutes • Heck Yeah! Free Time! – 15 minutes of free choice • Gum! Yum! – I can chew gum in class today • Incredible Me – I bring something for show and tell • Toasty Toes – I can wear slippers in class for a day • Buddy Pass – I can sit by a friend for a day • Holiday Shuffle – I can trade seats with a classmate for a day • Teacher’s Hat – I can wear a hat to class for a day • Dance Off – 5 minute dance party to the song of your choice • Lead the Way – line leader for the day • Lunch Date – Lunch with the teacher and a friend • Phone a Friend – positive phone call home • Ink For a Day – I can use a pen today • Give Me A Break – I can pick the class brain break • Santa’s Helper – teacher’s helper for a day • Laughter Pass – I can tell a joke to the class • Love My Device – I can play an educational game on my device • Classroom DJ – I can choose the music we listen to • Board Member – I can draw on the board for 10 minutes • Head of the Class – I get to sit at the teacher’s desk for a day • Wiggle Worm – I can walk around to get my wiggles out • King of the Mountain – I can sit on my desk while i work today • Back to the Drawing Board – I can draw for 15 minutes • Jail Break – I get my recess back • Cool Cat – I get to wear sunglasses for a day • Pick a Book – I get to pick the class read aloud • Teacher’s Toys – I can play with one of the teacher’s toys for 10 minutes • Borrower – I borrow one classroom item for a day • Hire Me Please – I get to pick my classroom job for the week • My Lovey – I get to bring my pillow or stuffed animal to class • I am so Crafty – make a craft and show it off to the class • Game Boy – I get to pick a game for the class to play • Picnic Lunch – eat lunch outside with the teacher and a friend • Substitute Teacher – take over teaching when the teacher is busy • Costume Party – I get to wear a costume to class for a day Wishing you the best year yet! – Matt and Tara

title: Plan a Vacation Project Based Learning PBL Design Projects Activities
description: Using Project Based Learning in your classroom is the perfect way to have your students incorporate reading, writing, math, critical thinking, and problem solving skills in a fun, real-life context. In this PBL unit, students will plan a vacation to Europe for them and their family! As they prepare for their adventure, they will create a realistic budget for their vacation. Give your students choice! As they work through this PBL, they will have the opportunity to make lots of decisions that will help to personalize their learning! In addition, I have included both a 5th and 6th grade version AS WELL AS a 3rd and 4th grade version. This provides a wealth of scaffolds so that you can differentiate in your classroom and ENGAGE EVERY LEARNER! THIS PROJECT BASED LEARNING ACTIVITY IS NO PREP! JUST PRINT AND GO! Please click the preview button, and/or watch the video to get a sense of how much your students will love this PBL experience. Included in this PBL project you will receive the following: Teacher PBL Notes PBL Design Elements Let’s Go to Europe Give Me Five The Final Three Map Your Route Country Planner Shopping at the Stuff Mart The Bare Necessities I’m Going to ______ Facts and Figures Must See Locations It’s My Jam Com Fly with Me (3rd & 4th AND 5th & 6th Version) We Get Around (3rd & 4th AND 5th & 6th Version) What to Do What to Do (3rd & 4th AND 5th & 6th Version) Put Us Up For the Night (3rd & 4th AND 5th & 6th Version) Authentic Cuisine (3rd & 4th AND 5th & 6th Version) Our European Vacation Websites for Research Life in ______ Hello From Europe Snapshots of ______ My Travel Journal A Day in the Life KWL Cities of ________ Make a Travel Brochure Project Country Flip Book Project Project Reflections Project Critique IMPORTANT: The 5th and 6th Grade version of this PBL asks students to do some research to complete selected tasks. Useful website links have been provided. HOWEVER, if you choose not to have your students do online research, ALL NECESSARY SCAFFOLDS have been provided so that students can successfully complete this project without completing their own research. I know you and your students will have so much fun on this PBL adventure! title: Rainforest Project Based Learning PBL Habitats ELA Math Writing Activity
description: Using this Rainforest Project Based Learning in your classroom is the perfect way to have your students incorporate reading, writing, science, math, critical thinking, and problem solving skills in a fun, real-life context. In this PBL unit, your kids will love being SURVIVOR KID as they try to survive 5 days and nights in the rainforest! As they work to survive the rainforest, they will need to plan and budget, design a shelter, design a water filtration device, plan for animal and insect encounters, build a compass AND SO MUCH MORE! title: Spring Would You Rather Opinion Writing Questions April Prompts Google Slides
description: These “Would You Rather” opinion writing activities for Google Slides are the perfect way to keep your kids engaged this APRIL. Kids are always starting a conversation with the phrase, “Would you rather . . .?” As Spring gets underway and with all the other fun that the month of April brings, keep your kids writing with a smile as they make a decision between two different things and explain the their reasoning. I love “Would You Rather” questions. They are perfect for introducing or working on opinion writing as kids are asked to make a choice and explain why they made the choice they did. Sometimes the kids find the choice easy to make. Other times it is a close call! “Would You Rather” activities are very versatile. I like to use them as quick warm-ups, for mini-lessons, classroom polls, literacy centers, for early finishers, or for differentiation. This comes with 30 fun “Would You Rather” writing activities on Google Slides for your kids to enjoy. SOME QUESTIONS IN THIS PACK INCLUDE: Would you rather give up your favorite food for a year or candy forever? Would you rather live in a windmill or a lighthouse? Would you rather write a best-selling book or direct a blockbuster movie? Would you rather take cooking lessons or piano lessons? Would you rather have a jacuzzi in your bedroom or a secret room in your house? Would you rather own a losing professional sports team or a successful chain of restaurants? Would you rather ride the scariest rollercoaster or bungee jump off the highest bridge? Would you rather give up movies for the rest of your life or never read another book? Would you rather drink water from a glacier or a running spring? Would you rather have a super power you could only use for good or be able to time travel without limits? Would you rather live in a science fiction or fantasy universe? title: Spring End of the Year Would You Rather Questions Opinion Writing May Prompts
description: These “Would You Rather” opinion writing activities for Google Slides are the perfect way to keep your kids engaged as the end of the year approaches this MAY. Kids are always starting a conversation with the phrase, “Would you rather . . .?” As the school year winds down and with all the other excitement that the month of May brings, keep your kids writing with a smile as they make a decision between two different things and explain the their reasoning. I love “Would You Rather” questions. They are perfect for introducing or working on opinion writing as kids are asked to make a choice and explain why they made the choice they did. Sometimes the kids find the choice easy to make. Other times it is a close call! “Would You Rather” activities are very versatile. I like to use them as quick warm-ups, for mini-lessons, classroom polls, literacy centers, for early finishers, or for differentiation. This comes with 30 fun “Would You Rather” writing activities on Google Slides for your kids to enjoy. SOME OF THE QUESTIONS IN THIS MAY PACK INCLUDE: Would you rather be trapped in a blizzard or a tornado shelter? Would you rather draw a sketch or a color picture? Would you rather go the extra mile or pull a rabbit out of a hat? Would you rather pet sit or babysit? Would you rather eat chicken feet or pig knuckles? Would you rather smell like strawberries or peaches? Would you rather live near railroad tracks or a busy airport? Would you rather see a merman or a female yeti? Would you rather read the journal of JFK or MLK? Would you rather be attacked by a flock of seagulls or outrun a stampede of horses? title: November Thanksgiving Would You Rather Questions Opinion Writing Google Slides
description: These “Would You Rather” opinion writing activities for Google Slides are the perfect way to keep your kids engaged this NOVEMBER. Kids are always starting a conversation with the phrase, “Would you rather . . .?” As fall is in the air, and we all are counting our blessings as Thanksgiving approaches, keep your kids writing with a smile as they make a decision between two different things and explain the their reasoning. I love “Would You Rather” questions. They are perfect for introducing or working on opinion writing as kids are asked to make a choice and explain why they made the choice they did. Sometimes the kids find the choice easy to make. Other times it is a close call! “Would You Rather” activities are very versatile. I like to use them as quick warm-ups, for mini-lessons, classroom polls, literacy centers, for early finishers, or for differentiation. This comes with 30 fun “Would You Rather” writing activities on Google Slides for your kids to enjoy. SOME QUESTIONS IN THIS NOVEMBER PACK INCLUDE: Would you rather watch or play football after dinner? Would you rather spend Thanksgiving at home or volunteering? Would you rather only be able to talk like a turkey or only eat turkey ? Would you rather the world have less bugs or less snakes? Would you rather sing or dance in public? Would you rather be chased by a scarecrow or a flock of crows? Would you rather eat cranberry sauce or gravy? Would you rather grow sunflowers or pumpkins at your home? Would you rather bob for apples or find a needle in a haystack? Would you rather eat out more or eat at home more? Would you rather never be able to change your mind or never be able to make up your mind? Would you rather use a coconut or a watermelon to bowl with? Would you rather travel the world or own a fancy car? Would you rather have Thanksgiving 5 days in a row or once every 5 years? Would you rather eat brussels sprouts or turkey gizzards? title: Reproductive System and Puberty Human Body Systems Worksheets Puberty
description: Are you studying The Human Reproductive System, our amazing human body systems, or puberty in your classroom or with your kids? This jam-packed Reproductive System resource will inspire a sense of wonder in your students as they study all about the the male and female reproductive system, human anatomy, fertilization, puberty for boys and girls and more. Included in this pack you will find reading passages, organizers, task cards, interactive notebook flippable activities, posters, and more. Have fun studying the human body this year! DIFFERENTIATED LEARNING Two different versions of this product are included. You will receive one version for students in grades 4-6 and another version for more mature learners or students in grades 7-8 . The 4-6 version includes basic passages, graphics, and diagrams. In the 7-8 version you will find more complex passages and more detailed images and diagrams. IMPORTANT CONSIDERATIONS What This Resource Is This resource is part of our collection of elementary and middle school teaching packs on the different systems of the human body. It is designed to give basic information to students about the anatomy of the male and female body, the fertilization process, and changes that occur during puberty. What This Resource Is NOT This is not sex education curriculum or sex ed materials. As such, it does not address topics like sexual intercourse, arousal, contraception or birth control methods, or sexually transmitted infections. Please Preview All Materials We have included a version of this product for students in grades 4-6 and a version for students in grades 7-8. However, only you know what is an is not age-appropriate material for your students. I highly encourage you to read through any passage or thoroughly look over and review any material or video before placing it in front of your students to ensure that the material is presented in a way that is right for your students. World View This is NOT religious curriculum. There are no references to religion. However, the authors of this resource do hold the view that a life begins at conception. With this in mind, there are a few references to a new life being formed at conception. Again, please preview all materials prior to giving them to your students. It includes a fascinating reading passage all about Indiana Dunes, along with SIX engaging reading response activities to help students retain what they’ve learned. The reading response activities are designed to cater to different learning styles and include a Main Idea Activity, Summarization Writing Activity, 321 Learning Organizer, and a Key Vocabulary Activity. Teachers can choose the activities that best suits their students’ needs and learning style. As a BONUS, you will also receive 2 National Park Organizer activities that your students are sure to love! In addition to the reading passage and response activities, the pack also includes two assessment options to gauge student understanding. This will help teachers easily determine if their students have truly mastered the content. If you want to make learning about national parks fun and engaging, this reading passage pack is for you. With clear and concise content, students will be able to comprehend and retain the information presented. Use this reading passage pack to give your students a chance to learn about one of the America’s most fascinating National Parks: Indiana Dunes. It is ideal for teachers, homeschoolers, and anyone else looking to teach children about the beauty and importance of national parks. Grab this reading passage to create an unforgettable learning experience for your students! title: National Parks Collaborative Poster Bulletin Board National Park Coloring
description: Are you ready to celebrate America’s National Parks this year? Celebrate our beautiful National Parks and encourage your students to promote conservation with this beautiful and fun AMERICA’S NATIONAL PARKS collaboration coloring poster! As you get ready to learn about our National Parks, teach your students the value of teamwork and that we are all beautiful together! Print. Color. Cut. It’s That easy. You and your students will love displaying this personalized poster for classroom, bulletin board, or hallway decor! Included you will receive one fun 35-Piece collaboration poster activity for your students to complete! Each square piece measures 6″ by 6″. Your completed poster will be 35 inches wide by 49 inches high. title: Juneteenth Nonfiction Reading Comprehension Passage and Questions June Passage
description: This print and digital Juneteenth reading comprehension passage is a great way to maintain the rigor in your classroom while celebrating this important day. Designed specifically for 3rd-5th students, this reading passage is a great way to give your students opportunities to practice their reading comprehension skills, while gaining valuable knowledge about this important holiday. In addition to the passage, this packet provides 2 Assessment Options with test-like questions (multiple choice and short answer) that will challenge and enhance your students’ comprehension skills. The passage is thoughtfully designed to provide a variety of informational that promotes a deeper understanding of the holiday. You will also receive 4 text-based activities that will help your students practice important skills and better retain what they have learned. Additionally, the Print and Digital formats offer flexibility for different classroom settings, allowing for a seamless integration of technology. This packet is perfect for teachers seeking to enhance their students’ reading abilities while fostering an appreciation for the Juneteenth holiday this year! Your students will love the passage and activities and you will love watching them grow into strong and enthusiastic readers!
